q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Django's redirect() returns an HttpResponseRedirect object without performing actual network I/O, so it is safe to execute in a test environment.

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: The violation (open redirect) is observable by inspecting the Location header or url attribute of the returned HttpResponseRedirect object.

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: returns.kind is 'value' and returns.exprs contains 'redirect(lane)', meaning the HttpResponseRedirect object is directly returned and accessible in memory.
